column1: # COLUMN pl_defaultname: NEA-style planet name (host + letter
# COLUMN tic_id: TESS Input Catalog ID (e.g.
# COLUMN pl_orbpererr1: Orbital period upper unc. [days]
# COLUMN pl_orbpererr2: Orbital period lower unc. [days]
# COLUMN pl_tranmiderr1: Transit mid-time upper unc. [days]
# COLUMN pl_tranmiderr2: Transit mid-time lower unc. [days]
# COLUMN pl_tsystemref: Time system for transit mid-time (BJD_TDB)
# COLUMN pl_orbsmaxerr1: Semi-major axis upper unc. [AU]
# COLUMN pl_orbsmaxerr2: Semi-major axis lower unc. [AU]
# COLUMN pl_orbeccen: Orbital eccentricity (free only if a non-RM RV instrument is present and f_c/f_s float)
# COLUMN pl_orblper: Argument of periastron [deg] (reported when eccentricity is free)
# COLUMN pl_orblpererr1: Argument of periastron upper unc. [deg]
# COLUMN pl_orblpererr2: Argument of periastron lower unc. [deg]
# COLUMN pl_impparerr1: Impact parameter upper unc.
# COLUMN pl_impparerr2: Impact parameter lower unc.
# COLUMN pl_radjerr1: Planet radius upper unc. [R_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_radjerr2: Planet radius lower unc. [R_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_massj: Planet mass [M_Jup] (reported for transiting planets with non-RM RV and free K)
# COLUMN pl_massjerr1: Planet mass upper unc. [M_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_massjerr2: Planet mass lower unc. [M_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_msinij: Minimum mass m sin i [M_Jup] (used in lieu of pl_massj for non-transiting planets)
# COLUMN pl_msinijerr1: m sin i upper unc. [M_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_msinijerr2: m sin i lower unc. [M_Jup]
# COLUMN pl_trandeperr1: Transit depth upper unc. [%]
# COLUMN pl_trandeperr2: Transit depth lower unc. [%]
# COLUMN pl_trandurerr1: Transit duration upper unc. [hours]
# COLUMN pl_trandurerr2: Transit duration lower unc. [hours]
# COLUMN pl_ratdorerr1: a / R* upper unc.
# COLUMN pl_ratdorerr2: a / R* lower unc.
# COLUMN pl_ratrorerr1: Rp / R* upper unc.
# COLUMN pl_ratrorerr2: Rp / R* lower unc.
# COLUMN pl_trueobliq: True obliquity psi [deg] (reserved; empty unless derived)
# COLUMN rm_snr_combined: Combined matched-filter RM SNR following Kipping (2024); per-dataset SNR = sqrt(Delta chi^2) (with vs. without RM signal
column2: using effective error sqrt(err^2 + jitter)); multiple RM datasets combined in quadrature

column1: # COLUMN st_name: Star name (same as hostname)
# COLUMN host_star_flag: Always 1 (this row is the host star)
# COLUMN st_refname: Reference / publication identifier (placeholder "999" until assigned)
# COLUMN st_logg: Surface gravity log g* [cgs]
# COLUMN st_loggerr1: log g* upper unc. [cgs]
# COLUMN st_loggerr2: log g* lower unc. [cgs]
# COLUMN st_vsin: Projected rotation velocity v sin i* [km/s]
# COLUMN st_vsinerr1: v sin i* upper unc. [km/s]
# COLUMN st_vsinerr2: v sin i* lower unc. [km/s]
# COLUMN rv_flag: 1 if any RV data is present in the fit
# COLUMN tran_flag: 1 if any photometry is fit
# COLUMN peak_prob: For bimodal A_mstar systems: probability mass under this row's stellar-mass mode (Major row > Minor); 1.0 for single-mode systems
# COLUMN BinaryMultiStarFlag: Y if SOCat (May16SoCat.csv) lists any binary/multi-star indicator for this host TIC
